title: Bound session context and define historical compatibility behavior
summary: Implement the 0.6.0 runtime simplification that bounds the role of session context and formalizes historical compatibility behavior for the redesigned resumable transaction protocol. This ticket ensures that `session_id` remains useful for observability, correlation, and handoff continuity without becoming the primary correctness anchor for active-transaction resume decisions, while also defining how legacy histories, older session assumptions, and pre-0.6.0 persistence behavior remain replayable under bounded compatibility rules.
outputs: Runtime behavior that treats `session_id` as bounded or secondary context rather than the primary correctness anchor for resumable transaction continuation
Updated session-related lookup and validation logic that preserves exact active-transaction continuation without making session-parent modeling mandatory for correctness
A documented compatibility policy for historical legacy transaction IDs, sentinel-bearing historical state, and older session-oriented persistence assumptions
Updated bounded compatibility behavior that preserves replayability of historical data without weakening strict new runtime semantics
Regression coverage for session-bounded correctness and historical compatibility behavior
acceptance_criteria: Runtime behavior does not require `session_id` to be the primary correctness anchor for resuming an active non-terminal transaction.
When present, `session_id` remains useful for observability, correlation, and bounded runtime context without overriding exact active transaction identity.
Resume and continuation logic prefer exact active transaction identity, canonical status, terminal state, and `next_action` over session-first selection in normal flow.
Session-related lookup or recovery logic no longer reintroduces heuristic active-transaction selection into the 0.6.0 runtime model.
Historical legacy transaction IDs remain replayable as opaque historical identifiers.
Historical data that reflects older session-oriented assumptions remains readable under bounded compatibility handling without becoming the canonical model for new runtime behavior.
Historical sentinel-bearing data, if present, remains bounded to compatibility handling and does not restore sentinel semantics to normal runtime flow.

Historical compatibility handling preserves readability of terminal materialized snapshots, including cases where terminal transaction context remains materialized before any post-terminal active-slot clearing behavior is applied.

Historical compatibility handling treats post-terminal active-slot clearing such as `tx.clear_active` as a bounded materialization concern and must not reinterpret it as a new lifecycle event or a replacement transaction.
No new runtime write path treats planning artifacts, legacy session assumptions, or compatibility shims as canonical execution truth.

Regression tests cover at least: session-present continuation, session-absent continuation where active transaction state is sufficient, historical legacy replay, and bounded compatibility behavior for older persistence assumptions.
The implementation preserves canonical lifecycle ordering, resumability, replay integrity, explicit terminal-state handling, and the single-active-transaction invariant.
tasks: Identify runtime code paths where session context currently participates in correctness-sensitive resume, lookup, or continuation behavior.
Update runtime behavior so exact active transaction identity remains the primary anchor for resumable correctness, with session context treated as bounded or secondary context.
Ensure session-present and session-absent continuation paths both preserve the same exact active transaction semantics when canonical transaction state is sufficient.
Update any session-related recovery or lookup logic so it does not override exact active-transaction continuation in normal flow.
Define and implement bounded compatibility handling for historical data that reflects older session-oriented assumptions.

Define and implement bounded compatibility handling for legacy transaction IDs, historical terminal materialized snapshots, and any historical sentinel-bearing material that may still appear in replayed data.
Ensure compatibility logic remains read-oriented for old histories and does not weaken the strict new-write rules of the 0.6.0 runtime model.
Update validation and error behavior so ambiguous or malformed historical compatibility inputs fail clearly when no safe deterministic continuation is possible.
Add or update regression tests covering session-bounded correctness, session-optional continuation, historical replay, and compatibility behavior for older persistence assumptions.
Document the bounded role of `session_id` and the bounded role of historical compatibility logic so future changes do not re-promote them into the primary correctness model.
notes: This ticket is runtime implementation work, not only contract-definition work.
The most important outcome is that session context remains useful without becoming the canonical source of truth for resumable correctness.
This ticket should preserve the transaction-centered design by preventing session-related recovery logic from reintroducing ambiguity into active-transaction continuation.
Historical compatibility matters because older logs and materialized state may encode assumptions that no longer define the 0.6.0 model.
The implementation should keep compatibility behavior bounded, explicit, and read-oriented rather than letting it leak into new runtime semantics.

session_role_rules.bounded_role: Session context may support observability, correlation, handoff continuity, and bounded runtime lookup assistance, but it must not become the primary correctness anchor for resumable continuation.
session_role_rules.primary_correctness_anchor: Exact active transaction identity plus canonical transaction state plus `next_action`.
forbidden_normal_flow_examples: treating missing or mismatched session context as permission to guess a different active transaction
promoting session-parent assumptions over exact active transaction state

compatibility_rules.legacy_tx_ids: Historical legacy transaction identifiers remain replayable as opaque historical state.
compatibility_rules.legacy_session_assumptions: Historical data that encodes older session-oriented assumptions remains readable only under bounded compatibility handling.
compatibility_rules.legacy_terminal_snapshots: Historical terminal materialized snapshots remain readable as bounded compatibility input, including shapes where terminal transaction context remains materialized before active-slot clearing occurs.
compatibility_rules.legacy_tx_clear_active_behavior: Historical or transitional `tx.clear_active`-style active-slot clearing behavior must be treated as a bounded materialization compatibility concern and must not be promoted into a replacement canonical lifecycle event.
compatibility_rules.legacy_sentinel_data: Historical sentinel-bearing state may be handled only as bounded compatibility input and must not redefine new runtime semantics.
compatibility_rules.no_forced_rewrite: Forward migration does not require in-place rewrite of historical logs or materialized artifacts.
compatibility_rules.new_write_policy: New runtime behavior must remain transaction-centered, exact-identity-first, and sentinel-free even when compatibility handling exists.

error_behavior.ambiguous_historical_input: Fail clearly when historical compatibility input is ambiguous and no safe deterministic continuation target exists.
error_behavior.malformed_historical_input: Fail clearly when malformed historical compatibility input cannot be reconciled safely.
error_behavior.design_goal: Prefer bounded compatibility replay or explicit failure over silent heuristic continuation.

test_focus: session-present continuation does not override exact active transaction identity
session-absent continuation still succeeds when canonical transaction state is sufficient
older session-oriented histories remain bounded to compatibility handling
historical terminal snapshots remain readable without reactivating terminal work
`tx.clear_active`-style compatibility handling does not create a replacement lifecycle event
compatibility logic does not reintroduce heuristic active-transaction selection

design_bias.preferred_anchor: Transaction-centered resumable correctness with session as bounded secondary context.
avoid: using compatibility behavior as the normal model for new runtime flow
reintroducing heuristic recovery through legacy shims
treating observability metadata as canonical execution truth

canonical_alignment.synchronization_intent: Use this ticket to keep session context bounded and historical compatibility explicit while preserving the 0.6.0 transaction-centered resumable protocol.
canonical_alignment.current_canonical_constraint: Implementation must preserve exact active transaction continuation as the canonical resumable path and must keep compatibility behavior bounded so it does not weaken new runtime semantics.
